failure_modes.text: Confirmation bias can make the current deal look stronger by filtering the real quality of the walk-away option.
failure_modes.mitigation: Enforce the dialectic: challenge every favorable assessment of the deal with its antithesis and require independent evaluation of the walk-away option.

failure_modes.text: Overemphasis on the base case can hide how bad the downside becomes if the preferred negotiation path fails.
failure_modes.mitigation: Stress-test assumptions against outcomes one or two standard deviations from the mean; incorporate reference-class history of similar negotiations.

failure_modes.text: Premature execution can rush the team into action before the real fallback path and constraints are clear.
failure_modes.mitigation: Promote deliberation before action: distinguish between values and beliefs, map constraints explicitly, and define the BATNA threshold before entering negotiation.

premortem_questions.text: What would we have to believe to accept a contrasting viewpoint, and are we truly open-minded enough to consider it?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would we have to believe to accept a contrasting viewpoint, and are we truly open-minded enough to consider it?

premortem_questions.text: Is the worst case bad enough?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

premortem_questions.text: What specific assumptions underlying this framework or alternative are not being met in our current reality?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What specific assumptions underlying this framework (or alternative) are not being met in our current reality?

heuristics.text: Use MECE to compare alternatives cleanly instead of letting options blur together.
Model competitive responses with game theory before treating the current deal as the only serious branch.
Keep a one-day answer so the best current fallback path is always coherent enough to act on.

curation_notes.summary: Because BATNA is proxy-derived rather than explicitly named in the raw file, the chunks stay conservative and stick to clearly supported fallback, comparison, and downside-discipline content.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor material that treated BATNA as more explicit in-source than the local raw file supports.
open_questions: This model is semantically useful but less source-explicit than most of the batch, so later adoption should keep that caveat visible.

allies: The raw allies section is strong on systems thinking, scaffolding, and symphony-style synthesis for later relation work.
antagonists: The raw antagonists section provides future relation value around inside view, anchoring, and curse of knowledge.
structured_tensions: The raw source explicitly preserves tensions against optionality, commitment-bias, and confirmation-bias.
